title: Vendor Metric
description: A vendor-defined metric available on a product, beyond the closed `available-metric.json` enum. Used in `reporting_capabilities.vendor_metrics` to declare proprietary metrics like Adelaide `attention_units`, Scope3 `gco2e_per_impression`, or Nielsen DAR demographic reach. The vendor's `brand.json` is the canonical anchor: agent capabilities (when measurement-agent surfaces ship), human-readable description, and metric documentation are resolved from `brand.json` `agents[type='measurement']` rather than duplicated inline. Promotion path: when the industry converges on a metric (e.g., via the IAB Attention Task Force), the spec adds it to the closed `available-metric.json` enum and the vendor extensions become historical aliases. The `standard_reference` field anchors that promotion to published standards rather than ad-hoc vendor convergence counts.

vendor.description: Vendor that defines and computes this metric. The vendor's `brand.json` is the discovery anchor for the measurement agent (entry with `type: 'measurement'` in the `agents[]` array) — agent_url is NOT inlined here to avoid duplicating the canonical record.

metric_name.description: Metric name within the vendor's vocabulary (e.g., `attention_units`, `gco2e_per_impression`, `dar_age_25_54`). Lowercase with underscores so a future enum promotion is a literal string lift. When multiple vendors converge on the same `metric_name`, buyers can do cross-vendor matching with no vendor pin via `required_vendor_metrics` — but cross-vendor querying via `metric_category` is more reliable in practice since vendors rarely converge on names before standardization.

metric_category.description: Coarse classification for cross-vendor discovery. Buyers query by category (e.g., `metric_category: 'attention'`) to match products that report ANY vendor's attention metric, regardless of metric_name.

standard_reference.description: Reference URI to a published measurement standard this metric implements (e.g., IAB Attention Measurement Guidelines, MRC Viewable Impression Measurement, GARM emissions framework). Anchors promotion of vendor extensions into the standard `available-metric.json` enum on standards-body publication, not on vendor-count thresholds.
